meaning: adj.(经常)没有钱的,身无分文的,贫穷的
examples: Cecil Fridjohn, my father, came from an impecunious Irish Jewish family.
They are independent, impecunious and able to tolerate all degrees of discomfort.
Maybe there's a room available in a large house inhabited by other impecunious twentysomethings.
The first month of the year is when Iranians, after overspending for Nowruz, feel most impecunious.
Calling all impatient and impecunious fans of East Asian fare in New York City: Food Gallery 32 is cooking in the midst of Koreatown.
